questions.question_text: Researchers continue to 'uncover' new insights about how fear shapes our daily lives. What does 'uncover' mean?

options.text: To hide and conceal important findings from the public
To create and invent entirely new ideas without evidence
To discover and reveal something that was previously unknown
To confirm and prove findings that were already well established

questions.explanation_ar: uncover تعني يكشف ويكتشف، أي إظهار شيء كان مخفياً أو غير معروف من قبل.

questions.question_text: Fear can both protect and 'hinder' us in unexpected ways. What does 'hinder' mean?

options.text: To support and assist someone in achieving their goals
To slow down and make progress more difficult
To encourage and motivate someone to try harder
To analyse and measure the effects of a situation

questions.explanation_ar: hinder تعني يُعيق ويُبطئ، أي جعل التقدم أو تحقيق الأهداف أكثر صعوبة.

questions.question_text: We sometimes 'encounter' threats that trigger a fear response before we understand them. What does 'encounter' mean?

options.text: To avoid and deliberately stay away from something
To study and carefully research something over time
To come across and meet something, often unexpectedly
To create and bring something new into existence

questions.explanation_ar: encounter تعني يواجه ويصادف، أي الالتقاء بشيء أو موقف غالباً بشكل غير متوقع أو مفاجئ.

questions.question_text: The brain processes fear faster than our conscious mind can 'comprehend' it. What does 'comprehend' mean?

options.text: To forget and dismiss information without processing it
To physically sense and detect something in the environment
To fully understand and make sense of something
To predict and anticipate something before it happens

questions.explanation_ar: comprehend تعني يستوعب ويفهم تماماً، أي إدراك معنى شيء ما بشكل كامل ومعمّق.

questions.question_text: People who 'overcome' their fears often develop stronger pathways for courage. What does 'overcome' mean?

options.text: To surrender and accept defeat when facing a challenge
To avoid and escape from a difficult situation entirely
To successfully deal with and defeat a difficulty or fear
To ignore and pretend a problem does not exist

questions.explanation_ar: overcome تعني يتغلب ويتجاوز، أي النجاح في مواجهة صعوبة أو عقبة والخروج منتصراً عليها.

questions.question_text: When fears 'interfere' with professional goals, they prevent people from achieving what they want to accomplish.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. interfere تعني يتدخل ويُعيق، أي أن المخاوف تُشكّل عائقاً يحول دون تحقيق الأهداف المهنية أو الشخصية.

questions.question_text: When people 'dwell on' their fears, they think about them repeatedly, which actually makes those fears stronger.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. dwell on تعني يتمحور حول ويفكر باستمرار في، وهذا يُقوّي المسارات العصبية للخوف ويجعله أشد حسب ما ذكره النص.

questions.question_text: People who choose to _____ their fears rather than avoid them often experience significant personal growth.
questions.explanation_ar: confront تعني يواجه بشجاعة، أي التعامل المباشر مع الخوف بدلاً من الهروب منه — وهو ما يُؤدي إلى النمو الشخصي.

questions.question_text: Convert to reported speech: Dr. Al-Mansouri said, 'Fear feeds on avoidance.'

options.text: Dr. Al-Mansouri said fear feeds on avoidance.
Dr. Al-Mansouri said that fear fed on avoidance.
Dr. Al-Mansouri said that fear will feed on avoidance.
Dr. Al-Mansouri told fear feeds on avoidance.

questions.explanation_ar: في الكلام المنقول الماضي نُحوّل: said + that + تحوّل الزمن للخلف. المضارع البسيط (feeds) → ماضٍ بسيط (fed). الخيار b صحيح.

questions.question_text: Convert: The researcher said, 'I am studying the amygdala's role in fear.'

options.text: The researcher said that he is studying the amygdala's role.
The researcher said that he was studying the amygdala's role.
The researcher told that he was studying the amygdala's role.
The researcher said that he studied the amygdala's role.

questions.explanation_ar: المضارع المستمر (am studying) يتحوّل في الكلام المنقول الماضي إلى ماضٍ مستمر (was studying). الخيار b صحيح. الخيار c خاطئ لأن told يحتاج مفعولاً به.

questions.question_text: Which sentence correctly uses 'tell' (not 'say') in reported speech?

options.text: She told that her research was complete.
She said her students that fear could be managed.
She told her students that fear could be managed.
She told that fear could be managed.

questions.explanation_ar: tell يحتاج دائماً مفعولاً به مباشراً (شخص) قبل that: tell + person + that. الخيار c صحيح: told her students that...

questions.question_text: What happens to 'will' when converting to reported speech with a past reporting verb?

options.text: It stays as 'will'
It changes to 'would'
It changes to 'should'
It changes to 'shall'

questions.explanation_ar: في الكلام المنقول الماضي تتحوّل الأزمنة للخلف: will → would، can → could، am/is → was، المضارع البسيط → الماضي البسيط.

questions.question_text: The reported speech version of 'She said: I can overcome my phobia' is: 'She said she could overcome her phobia.'
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. في الكلام المنقول: can → could، والضمير I → she. الجملة المنقولة صحيحة تماماً.

questions.question_text: Match each direct speech sentence to its correct reported speech version.

left_items: She said: 'Fear is a normal human emotion.'
He said: 'I will help my patients overcome phobias.'
The researcher said: 'We are making progress with VRET.'
Dr. Ahmed said: 'The treatment can change brain patterns.'
right_items: The researcher said that they were making progress with VRET.
She said that fear was a normal human emotion.
Dr. Ahmed said that the treatment could change brain patterns.
He said that he would help his patients overcome phobias.

correct_answer.left: She said: 'Fear is a normal human emotion.'
correct_answer.right: She said that fear was a normal human emotion.

correct_answer.left: He said: 'I will help my patients overcome phobias.'
correct_answer.right: He said that he would help his patients overcome phobias.

correct_answer.left: The researcher said: 'We are making progress with VRET.'
correct_answer.right: The researcher said that they were making progress with VRET.

correct_answer.left: Dr. Ahmed said: 'The treatment can change brain patterns.'
correct_answer.right: Dr. Ahmed said that the treatment could change brain patterns.

questions.explanation_ar: تحوّلات الكلام المنقول: is→was، will→would، are making→were making، can→could. الضمائر تتغير أيضاً حسب السياق.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, where is the amygdala located in the brain?

options.text: In the front of the brain, behind the forehead
A small, almond-shaped region deep in the brain
In the outer layer of the brain called the cerebral cortex
At the base of the skull, near the spinal cord

questions.explanation_ar: وصف النص اللوزة الدماغية (amygdala) بأنها منطقة صغيرة تشبه اللوزة في الدماغ تتولى معالجة استجابات الخوف.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, what percentage of adults in major Middle Eastern cities struggle with fears that affect their lives?
questions.explanation_ar: ذكر النص أن البحث المُجرى في المدن الكبرى في الشرق الأوسط كشف أن حوالي 15% من البالغين يعانون من مخاوف تؤثر في أهدافهم المهنية أو الشخصية.

questions.question_text: Passage A says 'understanding fear is the first step toward mastering it.' What does this suggest?

options.text: Fear disappears automatically once a person understands its causes
Knowledge about fear is necessary but not sufficient for managing it
Awareness of how fear works empowers people to respond to it better
Only scientists who study the brain can successfully overcome fear

questions.explanation_ar: تُضمّن هذه العبارة أن الفهم العلمي لآليات الخوف يُمكّن الناس من اتخاذ قرارات أكثر وعياً وفاعلية حين يواجهون مواقف مخيفة.

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, Dr. Al-Mansouri is a neuroscientist at King Abdullah University of Science and Technology.
questions.explanation_ar: صحيح. ذكر النص أن الدكتورة سارة المنصوري عالمة أعصاب في جامعة الملك عبدالله للعلوم والتقنية (كاوست).

questions.question_text: According to Passage A, the amygdala can trigger a fear response within _____ milliseconds.
questions.explanation_ar: اقتبس النص من الدكتورة المنصوري قولها إن اللوزة الدماغية يمكنها إطلاق استجابة الخوف في غضون 12 ميلي ثانية — أسرع من تفكيرنا الواعي.

questions.question_text: Match each fear management technique from Passage A to its description.

right_items: helping people understand how their brains process fear
a mindfulness-based approach to reducing anxiety
a therapy that changes thinking patterns to manage fear
slowly introducing feared situations in a controlled way

correct_answer.right: a therapy that changes thinking patterns to manage fear
a mindfulness-based approach to reducing anxiety
slowly introducing feared situations in a controlled way
helping people understand how their brains process fear

questions.explanation_ar: ذكر النص ثلاث طرق رئيسية لإدارة الخوف: العلاج المعرفي السلوكي، التأمل، والتعرض التدريجي — إلى جانب تثقيف الناس بكيفية عمل أدمغتهم.
